title: Flower In The Crannied Wall
content:
Flower in the crannied wall,
I pluck you out of the crannies,
I hold you here, root and all, in my hand,
Little flower�but if I could understand
What you are, root and all, and all in all,
I should know what God and man is.
meaning:
"Flower In The Crannied Wall" is a quintessential example of Alfred Lord Tennyson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
